data.title: fix(view): suppress broken pipe warning and add auto-cleanup on detach

data.body:
LOW: Time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition

While benign in practice, there's a theoretical race window between checking agent count (line 1589-1595) and issuing shutdown (line 1601). A new agent could spawn during this window, causing unintended shutdown.

Impact: Low - requires precise timing and only affects local development tool
Likelihood: Very low - window is milliseconds, spawn requires user action
Recommendation: Consider atomic 'shutdown if empty' server operation in future refactor

INFO: Hardcoded session name safe from injection

The tmux session name is hardcoded as 'botty' (literal string), so there's no command injection risk here. The comment references bd-1tr for future unique naming - when implementing that, ensure session names are validated/sanitized if user-controlled.

Current state: Safe ✓
Future consideration: If session names become dynamic, validate against tmux session name rules

INFO: Error handling for shutdown request

The shutdown request uses  to intentionally ignore errors. This is appropriate here since:
1. If server is already dead, shutdown fails but that's the desired end state
2. User has already detached - no way to report error
3. Tmux session kill (line 1606) will clean up UI regardless

Consider adding debug logging for shutdown failures for troubleshooting.

INFO: Broken pipe detection correctly implemented

The fix properly distinguishes broken pipe errors from other I/O errors:
- Broken pipe → debug level (expected when tmux kills pane)
- Other I/O errors → warn level (potentially actionable)
- Non-I/O errors → warn level (potentially actionable)

This is the correct approach for expected vs unexpected error conditions. No security implications.

SUMMARY: Auto-cleanup feature security assessment

Overall security posture: GOOD ✓

Authentication/Authorization:
- Unix socket uses 0o600 permissions (owner-only access) ✓
- All requests implicitly authenticated by socket access ✓
- Shutdown operation appropriately restricted to socket access ✓

Resource Management:
- Cleanup prevents resource leaks (orphaned servers/sessions) ✓
- No denial-of-service vectors introduced ✓

Command Execution:
- No command injection risks (hardcoded literals) ✓
- .args() usage prevents shell interpretation ✓

Error Handling:
- Broken pipe correctly downgraded to debug ✓
- Shutdown errors appropriately ignored ✓

Minor Issues:
- LOW: TOCTOU race between count check and shutdown (theoretical, benign)

Recommendation: APPROVE with observations documented for future reference

data.reason: Acknowledged: TOCTOU race is theoretical and acceptable for local development tool. Window is milliseconds, requires precise timing to hit. If this becomes an issue in practice, can add atomic shutdown-if-empty operation in server.
Acknowledged: Intentional error suppression since user has detached. Server shutdown proceeds regardless of tmux cleanup success.
Acknowledged: Session name hardcoded to 'botty'. When implementing bd-1tr (unique session names), will ensure proper validation.
Acknowledged: Correct implementation distinguishing expected broken pipes from real errors.
Acknowledged: Overall security assessment positive. All concerns addressed.
